t_ch: 不管！我是在宇宙间流浪的牛仔，不会为任何事情停留！
t_en: Who cares! I am a cowgirl who roams the universe as I please! I won't let anything stop me!

t_ch: 祝你在这里过得愉快。
t_en: I wish you a happy journey.

t_ch: 不用你说，每个牛仔都会找到最适合自己的生存方式！
t_en: Of course I'll have a happy journey. We cowgirls always find the style of life that suits us best!

t_ch: 还是流梦礁适合我啊。自由，清净，没什么麻烦事。爽快！
t_en: I think the Dreamflux Reef suits me best. Free, peaceful, and free of trouble. It's refreshing!

t_ch: 我刚到这的时候，也尝试过重拾盗贼的老本行。可没过多久我就想通了，就算夺来了东西，也没法带回现实啊，那何必还要冒这个风险呢？
t_en: When I first got here, I tried picking up my old profession. But it didn't take me long to realize that I couldn't take any of the stuff I pilfered back to the real world. So, what's the point of taking that risk?

t_ch: 所以我现在就躺平咯。在这地方和大伙聊聊天，唠唠家族的闲话，也挺舒服的。
t_en: So, I'm out of the game now. I'm pretty cozy just chatting with people around here and gossiping about The Family.

t_ch: 哎，困在梦里的时间长了，反而变得有点向往安定了。
t_en: Ah, having been stuck in this dream for so long, I'm starting to long to settle down.

t_ch: 哎，情绪平静下来后，想想觉得还挺奇妙的。
t_en: Ah. Now that my emotions have settled, I realize that this is actually quite wonderful.

t_ch: 我以前是个牛仔，过着全宇宙到处乱跑的生活。现在，困在梦里的时间长了，反而变得有点向往安定了。
t_en: I used to be a cowgirl, spending my life running all over the universe as I pleased. Now, having been stuck in this dream for so long, I've started to long to settle down.

t_ch: 你想在流梦礁安居吗？
t_en: Do you want to settle in the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 我觉得很好。
t_en: I think that would be nice.

t_ch: 是吧。这地方和城里是没法比，可足够有人情味。大家互相照顾，日子还算过得去。
t_en: Yep. Though it can't compare to the big city, there's something more human about this place. Everyone takes care of each other and life isn't bad.

t_ch: 并且我就是看不惯家族，说得冠冕堂皇，什么同谐，什么以强援弱。结果呢，还不是自己住在亮闪的房子里，留其他人自生自灭。
t_en: Plus, I still can't stand The Family and their pretentious proselytizing of Harmony and how they are the strong defending the weak... How can they say that when they live in their glittering houses and leave everyone else to fend for themselves?

t_ch: …好，决定了。不管梦境以后变成什么样子，我都会留在这里，非得把流梦礁建设成所有人都能自由生活的地方！
t_en: ...Alright. I've decided. No matter what the Dreamscape becomes in the future, I will remain here. I want to construct the Dreamflux Reef into a place where everyone can live freely!

t_ch: 我要告诉塔利亚的那帮人，「盗贼公国」的理想还没有破灭！
t_en: I want to let the gang back in Talia know that the Kingdom of Banditry's ideals have not been extinguished!

t_ch: 可惜啊…梦里什么都有，但连夜空都是假的。我看不见星星。
t_en: It's a shame though... We have everything here in the dream, but even the sky is fake. I cannot see the stars.

t_ch: 虽说在这过得不错，虽说塔利亚也不是个什么好地方…但有时候啊，还是会怀念从前。
t_en: Although I don't have a bad life, and Talia really isn't some glamorous paradise... Sometimes, I still miss my past.

t_ch: 我是从遥远的星球来的，最喜欢做的事就是驾驶飞船在宇宙里到处乱转。真的，你没体验过可能不知道，自由，浪漫，无拘无束，我爱死那种感觉了。
t_en: I come from a distant planet. There is nothing I love more than flying my spaceship around the universe as I please. Honestly, if you haven't experienced it, you won't understand that freedom, romantic sensation, and feeling of being truly unrestrained. I love that feeling so much.

t_ch: 飞船航行的轨迹就像张网，星星就是网里活蹦乱跳的鱼。我从哪里经过，那附近的星星就都属于我。我来到，我看见，我拥有全部。
t_en: The trail my spaceship leaves behind is like a net, and the stars are like frolicking little fish. Wherever I go, those stars belong to me. I came, I saw, and I owned everything.

t_ch: 可惜啊…梦里什么都有，但连夜空都是假的。这里没有星星。
t_en: It's a shame though... We have everything here in the dream, but even the sky is fake. There are no stars here.

t_ch: 要是真让我碰上家族成员，我才不会给他们好脸色看！
t_en: If I ever come across a member of The Family, I'll make them regret it!

t_ch: 那个「家族」里真的还有正常人吗？
t_en: Is there anyone normal in that "Family"?

t_ch: 我没和他们正面接触过，但相关的事在流梦礁里听了不少。米哈伊尔为建设这颗星球做了这么多，现在却只能隐姓埋名地藏起来，就离谱！
t_en: I've never met any of them face-to-face, but I've heard a lot about them in the Dreamflux Reef. That Mikhail did so much work to construct this planet and now, he hides himself away in complete anonymity? Ridiculous!

t_ch: 要是真让我碰上家族成员，我才不会给他们好脸色看！
t_en: If I ever come across a member of The Family, I'll make them regret it!

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐呢？
t_en: What about Miss Robin?

t_ch: 啊。那位小姐算是例外吧，我和她说过话，挺温和的，对孩子们也很热心。
t_en: Ah. Miss Robin is an exception. I've spoken with her before. She was quite gentle and very passionate about children.

t_ch: 听说知更鸟小姐很早就离开了匹诺康尼，所以没染上多少家族的臭味…要是她想成为梦主，我肯定举双手赞成。
t_en: I heard that Miss Robin left Penacony a long time ago, so she was not infected with the foul stench of The Family... If she wanted to become the Dreammaster, I would be in full support.

t_ch: 加拉赫先生呢？
t_en: What about Mr. Gallagher?

t_ch: 加拉赫啊…这个人很神秘，总能给你种熟悉、却又看不透的感觉，但交个朋友是没啥问题——而且他的调饮技术简直绝了，塔利亚的小酒馆根本没法比！
t_en: Gallagher... He's real mysterious. I can never read him, but he always seems strangely familiar, too. I guess he's alright as a friend... Plus, he mixes an unbelievable drink. The taverns in Talia could never compare!

t_ch: 哎，最近挺少在流梦礁碰到他，希望那些该死的家族走狗不会为难他。
t_en: Ah, I barely ever run into him around the Dreamflux Reef anymore... I hope those Family dogs don't go giving him any trouble.

t_ch: …它在动！看到了吗，它在动！
t_en: ...It's moving! Did you see that? It's moving!

t_ch: 什么？不就是个垃圾箱吗……
t_en: What? That's just a trashcan...

t_ch: 啊…哈哈、你也看不到…没关系，我看得到，我认得出危险……
t_en: Ah... Haha, you can't see it either... That's fine. I can see them, and I know the danger...

t_ch: 你发什么疯？别挡路……
t_en: What madness are you spouting? Get out of the way...

t_ch: 发疯？哈…发疯，我才没疯！而且，我告诉你，我才是清醒的那个！你们…都被家族骗了！
t_en: Madness? Hah... Madness, I'm not mad! I am the only one who sees what's going on here! You... you've all been lied to by The Family!

t_ch: 我告诉你，他们的爪牙无处不在…可能在那儿！也可能在…那儿！无时无刻不在监视着、监视所有人…阴魂不散！
t_en: I'm telling you, their minions are everywhere... They could even be right here! Or maybe... over there! They are always watching, they're watching all of us... They linger around every corner!

t_ch: …我先走了。你也最好离他远点，这人多半是偷渡来的，在流梦礁待久了就会成这样。
t_en: ...I'm getting out of here. You should stay away from him, too. He probably came here as a stowaway, spent too long in the Dreamflux Reef, and ended up like this.

t_ch: 家族…家族…他们永远盯着你！
t_en: The Family... The Family... They're always watching!

t_ch: 试图向他搭话。
t_en: Try to speak with him.

t_ch: 男人自顾自沉浸在自己的世界中，对你的试探毫无反应。
t_en: The man is lost in his own world, and he has no reaction to your inquiry.

t_ch: 停止试探。
t_en: Give up on speaking to him.

t_ch: 家族…哈哈…家族……
t_en: The Family... haha... The Family...

t_ch: 所有人都觉得我是个疯子…但我发誓，我看到了…我全都看到了，真的！
t_en: Everyone thinks I am insane... but I swear I saw it... I saw all of it, honestly!

t_ch: 你…愿意相信我吗……
t_en: Will you... believe me?

t_ch: 你去过美梦之外的地方吗？我不是说这座城寨…而是那种危机四伏的*噩梦*。
t_en: Have you ever been outside the dream before? I don't mean this fortress... I mean the nightmare fraught with danger.

t_ch: 我去过一次，朋友。我有一次误入了筑梦边境某处的「边界」——那是一道像是梦泡组成的高墙…人可以轻而易举地穿过去。
t_en: I've been there once, my friend. I once accidentally entered a "boundary" on the Dream's Edge by mistake... It was like a giant wall built from dream bubbles... People can pass through it quite easily.

t_ch: 我不知道该怎么形容那个地方…它就像是「黄金的时刻」的翻版，但…是活的。没错，字面意义上的「活的」。
t_en: I don't know how to describe that place... It was like a duplicate of the "Golden Hour," but... It was alive. That's right, exactly what it sounds like... It was "alive."

t_ch: 地面是…紫红色，很有弹性，时不时还会轻微颤动…墙面也是，我没敢碰，但它们看起来温热又黏腻…有点恶心。
t_en: The ground was... it was purplish-red and springy. It would shiver slightly from time to time... The walls too. I didn't dare touch them, but they looked warm and sticky... It was disgusting.

t_ch: 而且我还看到有其他人走了进去…呃，或许是人吧。然后那幢房子开始蠕动，就像是在…消化。我等了很久，那个人始终没有出来。
t_en: Then, I saw someone enter it... Um, they might have been a person. Then, the house began to squirm, and it was like it was... digesting? I waited and waited, but that person never came back out.

t_ch: 然后，我的身边突然出现了一群…钟表小子，金属制的。它们已经严重锈蚀了，从表情到身体都歪歪扭扭…它们的叫声尖锐刺耳，就好像用指甲刮钢板一样…呃。
t_en: Then, a group of Clockies suddenly appeared around me... They were all made of metal. They were heavily rusted, and everything from their expressions to their bodies was warped and twisted... Their cries were ear-splitting, like nails on a chalkboard... Ergh.

t_ch: 我吓坏了。按理说，我应该会吓醒的…但你经历过吗？ 就是你明知自己在做梦，想要醒来却动弹不得。到处都是怪物，我只能逃——
t_en: I was terrified. Normally, I should have woken up from the fear but... Has that ever happened to you before? You know you're dreaming, and you want to wake up but you're paralyzed. You're surrounded by monsters and all you can do is run...

t_ch: 男人如鲠在喉，再也吐不出半个音节，只兀自大喘着气，像极了报废的鼓风机。
t_en: The man has become too upset to speak. He is fighting just to get a single syllable out. All he can do is fight for air, like a punctured air pump.

t_ch: 他的眼中满是恐惧。
t_en: His eyes are filled with fear.

t_ch: 我经历了的这些事…一定都是家族布下的险恶阴谋！
t_en: These things that I experienced... They must have been some sinister plot set up by The Family!

t_ch: 哎…简直难以置信…但家族一定是想要对我*洗脑*……
t_en: Ah... It's hard to believe but... I know The Family must be trying to brainwash me...

t_ch: 你根本想不到那个鬼地方有多可怕！我费了好大的力气才从那片地狱里爬出来，结果…结果他们竟然对我说，我看到的全都是「忆质过敏诱发的幻觉」！
t_en: You can't even imagine how terrifying that place was! It took so much energy just for me to drag myself out of that hell! In the end... in the end, they told me everything I saw was "hallucinations triggered by memoria allergy"!

t_ch: 后来没过多久，他们另派了人过来照看我。这帮人的说法又变了…他们说那片诡异的梦境是个*普通*的「负面情绪回收站」…哈、哈哈……
t_en: Not long after, they sent people to come and look after me. These people said something completely different... They said that strange Dreamscape was just a normal "recycling center for negative emotions"... Ha. Haha...

t_ch: 这不是自相矛盾吗？我就觉得他们在骗我…他们肯定在酝酿一个大阴谋！我本打算把这些事情公之于众…可他们竟打算以「疗养」的名义软禁我，还密谋要消除我的记忆！
t_en: Isn't this just a contradiction? I feel like they're trying to fool me... They must be cooking up some huge conspiracy! I was going to take this information public... But they put me under house arrest in the name of "treatment" and planned to erase my memory!

t_ch: 好、好在我逃出来了…逃到了这片流梦之地。美梦并不安全…美梦就是个谎言！
t_en: F—Fortunately, I escaped... escaped to this land of Dreamflux. Dreams are not safe... Dreams are a lie!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，都去死吧！
t_en: Hahaha, we're all going to die!

t_ch: 嘿，朋友，你知道「美梦剧团」吗？就是那些梦里随处可见的小畜生……
t_en: Hey, my friend, do you know the "Sweet Dreams Troupe"? They're the little wretches you can see all over the place in the dream...

t_ch: 告诉你，你可别被它们无害的外表给骗了！它们都是吃噩梦长大的怪胎，家族邪恶计划的一部分！
t_en: Let me tell you, don't let their harmless appearance fool you! They are freaks that feed on nightmares to grow! They're part of The Family's wicked scheme!

t_ch: 想活命的话就离他们远点，可别被骗进噩梦里当肥料…要是那玩意主动招惹你，你就…就敲碎它们的壳子、打烂里面的怪物、将它们捣成泥、烧成灰……
t_en: Stay away from them if you want to live. They'll trick you into going into the nightmares and use you as fertilizer... If those things come at you, you've gotta... You've gotta smash their shells, beat the hell out of the monsters inside, grind them into paste, and burn them to ash...

t_ch: 我们得拯救匹诺康尼…和我一起把那些怪物赶回噩梦吧，让它们再也不能出来害人！
t_en: We have to save Penacony... Help me fight off those monsters together. We have to chase them back into the nightmare and make sure they can't come out and harm people again!

t_ch: 男人目光涣散，憔悴的脸庞被失了神智的狂喜涂满。
t_en: The man's eyes are unfocused and his pallid face is covered in the ecstasy of insanity.

t_ch: 哈哈…再见了，虚假的美梦！我逃出来了…我成功了，流梦之地会保护我的！
t_en: Haha... Farewell, fake dream! I have escaped... I succeeded! The land of Dreamflux will protect me!

t_ch: 再见吧，该死的怪物、肮脏的魔鬼！祝你们在地狱里永远发烂发臭！别想抓到我，更别想把我变成你们的点心…你们抓不到我的！
t_en: Farewell, cursed monsters and foul demons! May you rot in hell for all eternity! Don't even dream of trying to catch me or turn me into your little snack... You will never catch me!

t_ch: 哇哦…流梦城寨还真是个好地方啊。这气氛，这打光——都是素材！好久没像现在这样思如泉涌了，爽。
t_en: Wow... Dreamflux Fortress is truly wonderful! This atmosphere, this lighting... It is all material! I have not felt my thoughts rush like this in some time. It feels amazing!

t_ch: 可惜路上都是正常人，要是能再多点迷因怪物就好了……
t_en: It's just a shame that everyone I met along the way was so normal... If only I could have encountered a few more memetic monsters...

t_ch: <size=28>再来抓我一次吧，甜蜜的「死亡」！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Come and seize me once more, sweet "Death"!</size>

t_ch: 你好，我是「死亡」。
t_en: Hello, I am "Death."

t_ch: 「死亡」？！…什么嘛，你和那只怪物长得一点都不像。
t_en: "Death"!? ...What? You look nothing like that monster.

t_ch: 这是行为艺术吗？
t_en: Is this a performance art piece?

t_ch: 不、不好意思！我只是来这边采采风，打扰到您了……
t_en: Ah, S—Sorry! I'm just here collecting material. I didn't mean to disturb you...

t_ch: 您是…流梦礁本地人？还是误入的游客？能请您分享一下美梦以外的恐怖…我是说有趣经历吗？拜托您了，这对我真的很重要。
t_en: Are you... a Dreamflux Reef native? Or are you a guest who stumbled upon this place by mistake? Would you care to share with me your terrifying... I mean interesting stories in life outside the dream? Please, this is very important to me.

t_ch: 要聊聊吗？
t_en: Do you want to chat?

t_ch: 当然！稍等，我找下纸笔……
t_en: Of course! Just a moment. Let me find my pen and paper...

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 呃，您问我？我就是个名不见经传的小漫画家…自娱自乐，画灵异恐怖题材的那种。
t_en: Oh, me? I'm just a small-time manga artist... I draw horror and supernatural-themed manga for my own entertainment.

t_ch: 这个题材太冷门，所以我没什么读者。不过，凡是因为我的作品聚在一起的朋友们都有共同话题——我们都成了好朋友。
t_en: This kind of stuff is not in high demand, so I don't have many readers. Although, friends who gather around my work all share a common topic.... We've all become very close.

t_ch: 这样倒也没什么不好…至少我还挺满意的。毕竟「林子大了什么鸟都有」…我不擅长处理太复杂的人际关系，只想好好创作，那种麻烦事还是算了吧。
t_en: There is nothing wrong with this... I'm quite satisfied, at least. After all, "the wider the net you cast, the more fish you catch..." I'm not great at dealing with complex social interactions. I just want to create, and avoid trouble as much as possible.

t_ch: 你都有什么作品？
t_en: What works have you made?

t_ch: 一般情况下，我只画自己喜欢的题材和作品。打个比方，会在午夜活过来的人偶啊，墨水变成的恶魔啊…之类的。
t_en: Normally I just draw works and themes that I enjoy myself. A puppet that comes to life at night, ink that transforms into demons... Things like that.

t_ch: 迷因怪物也是我的取材对象，所以先前我一直在探听「死亡」的都市传说…然后就误打误撞来到这儿了。
t_en: Memetic monsters are also a source of inspiration for me. I've been looking into an urban legend about "Death"... That search brought me stumbling here.

t_ch: 好在结果不错，新作的灵感正在我的脑海里源源不断地涌现——我有预感，撼动寰宇画坛的惊世名作即将在我笔下诞生！
t_en: Fortunately, this has worked out wonderfully! I can feel inspiration flooding my mind endlessly... I have a feeling that a masterpiece that will shake the artistic world is soon to be born from my pen!

t_ch: 只是…我该怎么回去呢……
t_en: The only problem is... How do I get back...?

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 啊？没什么可分享的吗？好吧……
t_en: Oh? You've got nothing to share with me? Okay then...

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 您慢走。如果遇到了什么恐怖…有趣经历，可以随时分享给我啊！
t_en: Take care. If you come across any terrifying— interesting experiences, you can always come back and share them with me!

t_ch: 我突然意识到一个严重的问题……
t_en: I suddenly realized a serious problem...

t_ch: 不出意外的话，我果然还是出意外了——虽然找到不少好素材，可我现在找不到回去的路了……
t_en: If things are as I expect, the unexpected has occurred... Though I have collected a great deal of inspiration, I have lost my way back from here.

t_ch: 算了，来都来了…干脆在这边好好逛逛，权当度假吧。
t_en: Forget it. I'm here now... I'll just take a good stroll around here, like I'm on holiday.

t_ch: 这…真的是我想要的人生吗？
t_en: Is this... really the life I want?

t_ch: 说真的，{M#哥们}{F#姐们}，这几天我在想…我干脆要不就住在这儿，不回去了。
t_en: If I'm honest, {M#mister}{F#miss}, I've been thinking the past few days... Maybe I should just live here and not bother going back?

t_ch: 说起来嘛，就是我家里那档事。我的兄弟姐妹不少，个个都是各行各业的领军人物。我嘛，就是搞搞自己喜欢的事业……
t_en: Now I'm talking about it, I have problems with my family. I have many siblings who are leading figures in various industries. Me? I'm just doing something I enjoy...

t_ch: 你懂的，虽然来不了多少钱，但我至少也吃饱穿暖，过得也挺开心。可父母就知道成天数落我，说我什么用都没有、什么事都做不好……
t_en: You understand. Even though I'm not making much money, I can eat and sleep soundly. I'm quite content. But, my parents are always criticizing me, telling me I'm useless, and I can't do anything right...

t_ch: 我受够了，真的受够了——但我有时候竟然还觉得他们说得有道理…就我现在这种状态，真的是能用*好*来形容的吗？
t_en: I've had enough. I really have... But, sometimes I do think that they have a point... Can I really call the state of my life now good?

t_ch: 有时候就…哎，心情挺复杂的。不知道路在何方，只能得过且过……
t_en: Sometimes I... Ah, it's really complicated. I don't know where my path is, so I just have to keep muddling along...

t_ch: 这…真的是我想要的人生吗？
t_en: Is this... really the life I want?

t_ch: 说真的，{M#哥们}{F#姐们}，这几天我在想…我干脆要不就住在这儿，不回去了。
t_en: If I'm honest, {M#mister}{F#miss}, I've been thinking the past few days... Maybe I should just live here and not bother going back?

t_ch: 说起来嘛，就是我家里那档事。我的兄弟姐妹不少，个个都是各行各业的领军人物。我嘛，就是搞搞自己喜欢的事业……
t_en: Now I'm talking about it, I have problems with my family. I have many siblings who are leading figures in various industries. Me? I'm just doing something I enjoy...

t_ch: 你懂的，虽然来不了多少钱，但我至少也吃饱穿暖，过得也挺开心。可父母就知道成天数落我，说我什么用都没有、什么事都做不好……
t_en: You understand. Even though I'm not making much money, I can eat and sleep soundly. I'm quite content. But, my parents are always criticizing me, telling me I'm useless, and I can't do anything right...

t_ch: 有时候就…哎，心情挺复杂的。不知道路在何方，只能得过且过……
t_en: Sometimes I... Ah, it's really complicated. I don't know where my path is, so I just have to keep muddling along...

t_ch: 这…真的是我想要的人生吗？
t_en: Is this... really the life I want?

t_ch: 哼，这地方比十二时刻清净多了。
t_en: Hmph, this place is much more peaceful than the Twelve Hours.

t_ch: 说来倒是又好气又好笑：我来到流梦礁这些天，最大的收获竟然不是「灵感」，而是「清净」。
t_en: Speaking of, there's something quite funny and frustrating: The most valuable thing I gained from these few days in the Dreamflux Reef was not "inspiration" at all, but "peace."

t_ch: 前阵子我在美梦里取材，可以说是处处碰壁。那些沉迷美梦的体面人啊，我好声好气地请教，他们讲不出个所以然就算了，还要反过来教育我不懂美梦的规矩。
t_en: Before, when I would gather materials in the dream, you could say I was just banging my head into a brick wall. I'd politely ask these dream-obsessed, upstanding, moral people for advice, but they couldn't give me a straight answer. What's more, they'd go on a tirade about how I didn't understand the rules of the dream.

t_ch: 然后，他们就开始说车轱辘话，试图教会我上流社会的*礼仪*。等我学明白了这套组合拳，想要继续深挖创作素材时，才发现这已经是他们的毕生所学了。
t_en: Then, they'd talk in circles and attempt to teach me the manners of high society. After I understood that combo and tried to dig for more creative inspiration from them, I realized that this was all they had to show for their entire lives.

t_ch: 哎，美梦啊美梦…每个人都得穿上同一套精致的正装，遵守他们那套莫名其妙的规矩，不然就是没底蕴、没涵养。
t_en: Ah, this dream... Everyone must adorn themselves in the finest wear and follow these strange rules, or they're seen as uncultured and unrefined.

t_ch: 可我就是做点自己喜欢的事而已，要这些有什么用？简直太折磨了……
t_en: But, I just want to do something I enjoy. Why do I need these things? It was simply torturous...

t_ch: 流梦城寨真是个好地方啊…嘿嘿。
t_en: Dreamflux Fortress is truly wonderful... Hehe.

t_ch: 在黄金的时刻呆久了，看流梦礁的垃圾桶都觉得眉清目秀…真的，我没开玩笑。
t_en: After spending all that time in the Golden Hour, even the trashcans of the Dreamflux Reef start to look beautiful... No, I'm being serious.

t_ch: 你知道吗？刚来这边的时候，我看到餐厅竟然和垃圾站比邻，只觉得头皮发麻……
t_en: Did you know? When I first got here, I saw a restaurant right next to a waste collection station... It gave me the shivers...

t_ch: 但后来，我发现自己居然渐渐适应了…至少现在我能在路边席地而坐，面不改色，边和人聊天边吃东西了。
t_en: But, I gradually grew accustomed to all this... At least now I can sit on the roadside without making faces, and talk with people while eating.

t_ch: 而且说实话，那家餐厅的食物虽然卖相谈不上好，但味道还不错。流梦礁的生活条件虽然不怎么样，氛围可真是非常宜居了。
t_en: And if I'm honest, the food in the restaurant didn't look great, but it didn't taste bad at all. Though the living conditions in the Dreamflux Reef may be nothing to write home about, the atmosphere is actually very comfortable.

t_ch: 说起来，前阵子我在街边买到个小纪念品。瞧着挺可爱的，我就多买了几只——谢谢你陪我聊天，就送你一个作谢礼吧！
t_en: Come to think of it, I saw some small souvenirs in a street-side store a while ago. I thought they looked cute, so I bought a few extra... Thank you for talking with me. Have a thank-you gift.

t_ch: 在黄金的时刻呆久了，看流梦礁的垃圾桶都觉得眉清目秀…真的，我没开玩笑。
t_en: After spending all that time in the Golden Hour, even the trashcans of the Dreamflux Reef start to look beautiful... No, I'm being serious.

t_ch: 说实话，流梦礁的生活条件虽然不怎么样，氛围可真是非常宜居了。
t_en: If I'm honest, the living conditions in the Dreamflux Reef may not be anything to write home about. But, the atmosphere is not bad at all.

t_ch: 哟，瞧瞧是谁来了？{M#好小子}{F#好姑娘}，没想到我们居然在这又见面了。
t_en: Oh, look who it is! I didn't expect to meet you again, {M#boy}{F#girl}.

t_ch: 看来你已经发现了匹诺康尼美梦的秘密。无名客的本事果然不赖。既然已经知道了流梦礁的存在，现在你又如何看待这片流梦之地呢？
t_en: I see you've also discovered the secrets of Penacony's beautiful dream. I should know not to doubt the skills of a Nameless. Now that you have learned of the Dreamflux Reef, what do you think of this land?

t_ch: 它有存在的价值。
t_en: Its existence has value.

t_ch: 哈哈，我同意。有时候，我甚至觉得梦里比现实还要清醒呢。
t_en: Haha, I agree. Sometimes, I feel more awake in a dream than I do in real life.

t_ch: 梦境终究只是逃避的手段。
t_en: The Dreamscape is just another escape for people.

t_ch: 哈哈，不愧是强者的发言！不过嘛，普通人总会需要一个避风港的。
t_en: Haha, the words of the strong! For real though, normal people will always need a safe haven.

t_ch: 当然，你可以坚持自己的答案，只是我不认为一刀切是个好主意。就算没有美梦，人们也总能找到点什么来沉迷。
t_en: Of course, you can stick with your answer. I just don't think that this is a one-size-fits-all situation. Even if dreams did not exist, people would still find something to lose themselves in.

t_ch: 这道题对我来说有点超纲…
t_en: I think this question is a little outside my pay grade...

t_ch: 哈哈，好吧。至少你没有随便应付我，这份诚实值得认同。
t_en: Haha, fine. At least you didn't just give me some perfunctory response. I praise your sincerity.

t_ch: 既然如此，我来分享一下自己的看法吧。
t_en: In that case, allow me to share my views.

t_ch: 流梦礁不同于十二时刻，它的立身之本是「自由」。这里每个人都有故事，其中不乏与我相似的家伙。
t_en: What makes the Dreamflux Reef different from the Twelve Hours is that it was built upon the foundation of "freedom." Everyone here has a story, even someone like me.

t_ch: 依我看，「自由」的反面是「秩序」…而匹诺康尼梦境的两种形态便是这类争论的延伸。
t_en: If you ask me, the opposite of freedom is order... The two forms of Penacony's Dreamscape are but an extension of this argument.

t_ch: 十二时刻是根除了烦恼的真空，流梦礁则是不加修饰的原始梦境。它们就像硬币的正反面，缺一不可。我不清楚「钟表匠」创造流梦礁到底是为了什么，但……
t_en: The Twelve Hours is a vacuum that had eliminated all worries. The Dreamflux Reef is an unadulterated Primordial Dreamscape. They are like two sides of the same coin. One cannot exist without the other. I don't know why the Watchmaker created the Dreamflux Reef, but...

t_ch: 以开拓客的视角来看待…或许开拓之道的真谛，就是要有选择的余地。
t_en: As a Trailblazer... Perhaps the true meaning of the way of Trailblaze is to have the space to make decisions for yourself.

t_ch: 开拓，必须靠自己迈步向前，而非被鞭策或驱使。当你可以选择逃避时，勇敢才更显珍贵。
t_en: Trailblaze requires us to rely on ourselves to move forward, not to be spurred or urged in any direction. It is when you have the option to run away that courage can truly be considered valuable.

t_ch: 有人喜欢享乐，期盼它能冲淡现实的不快；有人厌恶繁华，渴望过无拘无束的生活…无论哪种人，在梦中都理应有容身之处。
t_en: Some people enjoy pleasure and hope, that it can dilute the misery of reality. Some despise opulence and long for a life without restraints... Whatever kind of person you are, there should be a place for you in the dream.

t_ch: 所谓「不可能之事」，就是鼓励我们做出选择，找到真正属于自己的美梦——哪怕它并不华丽。
t_en: That which we call "impossible" is what encourages us to make a choice and find the dream that truly belongs to us... Even if it is not glamorous.

t_ch: 这只是我个人的一点感想。当然，我可能只在第三层，而「钟表匠」在第五层…有机会能当面问问他就好了。
t_en: These are just my personal views. Of course, I imagine I am still only on something like the third floor when the Watchmaker is up high on the fifth... I would love to have a chance to ask him myself one day.

t_ch: 我还会在流梦礁待上一段时间。如果你有想法，可以随时来找我聊聊。
t_en: I will be in the Dreamflux Reef for some time yet. If you have any more thoughts, you can talk to me anytime.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 好啊，你起头吧。
t_en: Alright, you start.

t_ch: 我还想听你的故事。
t_en: I want to hear more of your stories.

t_ch: 经历了这么多之后，我的故事已经不足为奇了。不过既然你想听，我还是乐意讲讲的。
t_en: After all I've been through, I realize my stories are no longer remarkable. But, since you want to hear one, I'd be happy to tell you.

t_ch: 那么，你要听什么样的故事呢？
t_en: So, what kind of story would you like to hear?

t_ch: 你是如何来到流梦礁的？
t_en: How did you come to the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 说来话长。上次跟你分别之后，我又把线人的东西仔细看了看，还真琢磨出了些许玄机。
t_en: It's a long story. After we bid farewell the last time, I looked back over the informer's things and uncovered some secrets...

t_ch: 实际上，那群受害者都有一个共同之处——现在你已经知道它是什么了。不过我当时并没有直接想通，反倒因为过度思考而有些神志不清……
t_en: In reality, all of the victims had one thing in common... Now, you know what that is. But back then, I didn't put it together straight away. If anything, I overthought it so much that I ended up even more confused...

t_ch: 于是我直接在梦里闭目养神，睁开眼就发现自己到这来了。很神奇，不是吗？顶级的谜语有时只需要最简单的解题方式。
t_en: So, I closed my eyes to quiet my mind, and when I opened them, here I am. Isn't it mysterious? Sometimes the hardest of riddles are solved in the simplest ways...

t_ch: 还真是…巧啊。
t_en: That is... a coincidence.

t_ch: 这不是什么都没解出来嘛！
t_en: That didn't explain anything!

t_ch: 听上去是有些掉价，可许多宾客都是这样进入流梦礁的——他们的反应比我夸张多了。
t_en: It may be a little disappointing, but many guests entered the Dreamflux Reef in this way... though their reactions were a lot more dramatic than mine.

t_ch: 不过，要说会主动留在这里的家伙，到底都是些勇敢的人——毕竟，这种原始的梦境里可没有家族「不会发生危险」的承诺。
t_en: I have to say, the people who are actively choosing to stay here are extremely brave... A Primordial Dreamscape like this doesn't have the same Family guarantee of "being free from danger," after all.

t_ch: 你听说大剧院的事了吗？
t_en: Have you heard about what happened at the Grand Theater?

t_ch: 「秩序」的残党？死了那么久的星神，居然还能搞起事来，真是稀奇…呃，咳咳，我可不是在说咱们那位阿基维利。
t_en: The remnants of "the Order"? It truly amazes me that an Aeon that has been dead for so long could still cause issues... Ahem, I'm not talking about our Akivili, of course.

t_ch: 话说回来，这乱子之所以能顺利解决，肯定少不了你们星穹列车的一份功劳吧？
t_en: Come to think of it, this whole situation being resolved so smoothly must have been considerable thanks to your Crew, right?

t_ch: 那当然，我超勇的。
t_en: Of course, we're awesome.

t_ch: 马马虎虎啦，全是大家的功劳。
t_en: Sort of, everyone did their share.

t_ch: 都是自动战斗的功劳。
t_en: It's all thanks to auto-battle.

t_ch: 哈哈，星穹列车啊…真不愧是无名客最大的牌面。实不相瞒，你们名气越大，我们也就越跟着沾光。
t_en: Haha, the Astral Express... truly lives up to its reputation as Nameless' greatest asset. Truth be told, the more your fame grows, the more we bask in your glory.

t_ch: 指不定，哪天我甚至可以在宇宙间大摇大摆地逛荡，再也不用看其他人的脸色…嗐，希望我能活着见到那一天吧。
t_en: Who knows, maybe one day I'll be able to roam the vast universe as I please, without having to answer to anyone ever again... Ah, I hope I live to see that day.

t_ch: 之前跟踪我的人是你吗？
t_en: Were you following me just now?

t_ch: 嘿，{M#好小子}{F#好姑娘}，现在才察觉到吗？
t_en: Hey, {M#smart boy}{F#smart girl}, have you only just noticed?

t_ch: 其实，我不是没办法离开筑梦边境。只是我在银河里摸爬滚打这么多年，直觉告诉我这一趟必定凶险异常——家族可能早就有所防范了。
t_en: Truth be told, I have the means to leave the Dream's Edge, but I've spent enough time in the cosmos to instinctually know that the trip is going to be perilous. The Family may have already taken precautions.

t_ch: 所以我才打算让你先探探路，把家族为我布下的陷阱都踩干净。结果呢？我在第五层，家族在第一层。
t_en: That's why I wanted you to explore the route first so that you could trigger all the traps laid for me in advance. But, where did that leave me? I'm here on the fifth level, and The Family is on the first.

t_ch: 除了那俩骚扰我的公司喽啰外，什么麻烦都没有。啧啧，感觉还有点平淡呢……
t_en: Aside from the two IPC minions who messed with me, there were no other troubles. Tsk, it almost feels a little boring.

t_ch: 你在利用我？
t_en: You were using me?

t_ch: 哎，我确实没法否认这个指控，所以…能请你不要把它放在心上吗？
t_en: Ah, I can't deny that, but... I hope you aren't bothered by it.

t_ch: 当然可以。
t_en: Of course I can do that.

t_ch: 果然！象征「开拓」的无名客心胸也很「开阔」…感激不尽。
t_en: I knew it! Thanks for being the bigger person here, Nameless. I could not be more grateful to you.

t_ch: 不过，为表歉意，我还是送你个赔罪礼吧——拿好哦！
t_en: To show my remorse, here's a token of my apology. Please keep it!

t_ch: 我觉得不行…
t_en: I don't think that's okay...

t_ch: 我知道这种事很难被原谅，但…我至少没有伤害你，对吧？哈哈，对吧……
t_en: I know that something like this is not easy to forgive, but... at least I didn't harm you, right? Haha, right...

t_ch: …呃，抱歉，我知道错了。为表歉意，这个送你当赔罪礼吧。
t_en: ...Ah, I'm sorry. I know it was wrong. Please accept this gift as an apology.

t_ch: 好吧，下不为例。
t_en: Fine, just this once.

t_ch: 果然！象征「开拓」的无名客心胸也很「开阔」…感激不尽。
t_en: I knew it! Thanks for being the bigger person here, Nameless. I could not be more grateful to you.

t_ch: 不过，为表歉意，我还是送你个赔罪礼吧——拿好哦！
t_en: To show my remorse, here's a token of my apology. Please keep it!

t_ch: 就先聊这些吧。
t_en: Let's leave the topic here.

t_ch: 再见啦。说不定有朝一日，你也能成为船长呢，哈哈。
t_en: See you! Who knows, maybe one day I can become a captain, too! Haha.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见啦。说不定有朝一日，你也能成为船长呢，哈哈。
t_en: See you! Who knows, maybe one day I can become a captain, too! Haha.

t_ch: 「开拓」之道…呵呵，还真是深奥啊。
t_en: The way of the Trailblaze... Hah, how profound.

t_ch: 科琳收敛了情绪，用一抹微笑取而代之。
t_en: Colleen gathers her emotions and sheathes them with a smile.

t_ch: 待的时间一长，我也有些喜欢这个地方了——主要是喜欢那个巨大的忆质空洞——它让我想起了宇宙。
t_en: As time has passed, I've come to like this place... I enjoy the giant memoria void more than anything... It reminds me of the universe.

t_ch: 和「黄金的时刻」不同，流梦礁的夜晚更加平静。怎么说呢？就像在现实里熬夜似的。我的心也会跟着一起平静下来，得以好好感受夜晚…或者说太空，最原本的面貌。
t_en: The evenings of Dreamflux Reef are calmer and quieter than the Golden Hour. I'm not sure how to explain it. It's like staying up through the night in the real world. My heart falls calm alongside it, and I'm able to experience the true nature of the night fully... Or maybe I should say space.

t_ch: 据说，许多人都会像我这样，向夜色寻求慰藉。至于原因，用文艺一点的说法…大概就是「人总会向往星空」吧。
t_en: I hear many people seek solace in the night like I do. As for the reason, I guess a more literary explanation would be... "Humanity will always yearn for the stars."

t_ch: 在外面漂泊这么久…我也有些想念家乡了。
t_en: Having roamed the outside world for all this time... I'm starting to miss home, too.

t_ch: 科琳突然一言不发，神情落寞。看起来就像是一位…智慧的贤者。
t_en: Colleen suddenly falls silent as her expression becomes gloomy. Suddenly, she looks like... a wise sage.

t_ch: 不知不觉间…冒险就已经结束了啊。我花了好大力气才解开谜题，来到这里。但是现在，突然间我就无事可做了……
t_en: Before I even realized... my adventure had already ended. I went to great lengths to solve the puzzle and make it here. Now, I'm suddenly left with nothing to do...

t_ch: 接下来要去哪里呢，回到家乡吗？唉，不知道为什么，鼻子突然有点酸……
t_en: Where am I supposed to go from here? Back home? Ah, I don't know why, I'm getting a bit of a lump in my throat...

t_ch: 哈哈…一个人在外面漂泊这么久，搞得我都变得多愁善感起来了。
t_en: Haha... All this roaming the world alone has made me soppy and sentimental...

t_ch: 钟表匠…你到底在哪儿呢？
t_en: Watchmaker... Just where have you gone?

t_ch: 科琳皱起了眉，表情却并不夸张。
t_en: Colleen's face folds into a gentle frown.

t_ch: …这就是冒险的尽头吗？「钟表匠」想要向我们展现的，就只是这样的小场面而已？
t_en: ...Is this really the end of the adventure? Is this small stage really all that the Watchmaker wanted to show us?

t_ch: 怎么突然抱怨起来了？
t_en: Why are you complaining all of a sudden?

t_ch: 不，我倒没有抱怨，只是觉得有点…没劲。
t_en: No, I'm not complaining. I'm just a little... defeated.

t_ch: 之前不是还夸他来着吗？
t_en: Weren't you praising him just before?

t_ch: 确实，他的布局值得肯定，但我却感觉有点…没劲。
t_en: It's true that his plans deserve credit. I just feel a little... defeated.

t_ch: 原本我以为流梦礁会是整段冒险的高潮，高低得在这里发生一段激烈的打斗，然后感悟出宇宙真谛什么的……
t_en: I thought that Dreamflux Reef was going to be the crux of the entire adventure. I thought that I would be cast into fierce battle and come to understand the truths of the universe...

t_ch: …但是除了几位无名客的衣冠冢外，我什么都没见到。难道它就是「钟表匠的遗产」？未免也太无聊了！
t_en: ...But I've not seen anything here besides the empty tombs of a few Nameless. Are they really the Watchmaker's Legacy? That's such an anticlimax...

t_ch: 是有什么谜题没解开吗？还是说米凯有事瞒着我……
t_en: Is there some kind of puzzle we haven't yet solved? Or is Micah hiding something from me...

t_ch: 啧，钟表匠…你到底在哪儿呢？
t_en: Tsk... Where are you, Watchmaker...?

t_ch: 科琳皱起了眉，表情却并不夸张。
t_en: Colleen's face folds into a gentle frown.

t_ch: 原本我以为流梦礁会是整段冒险的高潮，高低得在这里发生一段激烈的打斗，然后感悟出宇宙真谛什么的……
t_en: I thought that Dreamflux Reef was going to be the crux of the entire adventure. I thought that I would be cast into fierce battle and come to understand the truths of the universe...

t_ch: …但是除了几位无名客的衣冠冢外，我什么都没见到。难道它就是「钟表匠的遗产」？未免也太无聊了！
t_en: ...But I've not seen anything here besides the empty tombs of a few Nameless. Are they really the Watchmaker's Legacy? That's such an anticlimax...

t_ch: 啧，钟表匠…你到底在哪儿呢？
t_en: Tsk... Where are you, Watchmaker...?

t_ch: 梦境给了我一个重新尝试的机会…我已经开始享受这种感觉了。
t_en: The Dreamscape has given me a chance to start again... I have already come to enjoy this feeling.

t_ch: 突然想起一件好笑的事情——我在这边给自己安排的假身份，是一个离家出走的叛逆少女。
t_en: I've just realized something funny... The false identity I gave myself here is that of a rebellious young girl that ran away from home.

t_ch: 很扯，对吧？但确实让我焕发了第二春。而且…我伪装得很好。人的心态会被他人所扮演的社会角色左右——没有什么比一位可怜的少女更容易惹人疼爱了。
t_en: Ridiculous, right? But, it's given me a second chance at youth. Plus... I play the role well. People's attitudes are influenced by another character's role in society... And what is more loveable than a pitiful young girl?

t_ch: 这让我想起…小时候的我躺在床上总是幻想，倘若我出生在另一个星球，会不会过上完全不同的人生？
t_en: This reminds me... When I was a child, I used to lie on my bed and fantasize. I wondered if I was born on another planet, would my life have been completely different?

t_ch: 可惜，现实没有如果。倒是这座梦境给了我个重新尝试的机会…我已经开始享受这种*扮演他人*的感觉了，哈哈。
t_en: Unfortunately, there are no hypotheticals in the real world. But it was this Dreamscape that actually gave me the chance to start again... And I've come to enjoy the feeling of playing another character... Haha.

t_ch: 突然想起一件好笑的事情——我在这边给自己安排的假身份，是一个离家出走的叛逆少女。
t_en: I've just realized something funny... The false identity I gave myself here is that of a rebellious young girl that ran away from home.

t_ch: 很扯，对吧？但确实让我焕发了第二春。而且…我伪装得很好。人的心态会被他人所扮演的社会角色左右——没有什么比一位可怜的少女更容易惹人疼爱了。
t_en: Ridiculous, right? But, it's given me a second chance at youth. Plus... I play the role well. People's attitudes are influenced by another character's role in society... And what is more loveable than a pitiful young girl?

t_ch: 这让我想起…小时候的我躺在床上总是幻想，倘若我出生在另一个星球，会不会过上完全不同的人生？
t_en: This reminds me... When I was a child, I used to lie on my bed and fantasize. I wondered if I was born on another planet, would my life have been completely different?

t_ch: 可惜，现实没有如果。倒是这座梦境给了我个重新尝试的机会…我已经开始享受这种*扮演他人*的感觉了，哈哈。
t_en: Unfortunately, there are no hypotheticals in the real world. But it was this Dreamscape that actually gave me the chance to start again... And I've come to enjoy the feeling of playing another character... Haha.

t_ch: 男人似乎对你的打扰表示不悦。他静静地看着你，过了好一会儿才开口。
t_en: The man seems displeased at you disturbing him. He stares at you silently for a considerable while before opening his mouth to speak.

t_ch: 知道流梦礁最大的优点是什么吗？清静。
t_en: Do you know what the greatest thing about the Dreamflux Reef is? The peace and quiet.

t_ch: 没有响个不停的喇叭，没有闪来闪去的灯光，也没有会走路的广告牌过来烦你。
t_en: There are no horns blaring constantly, no flashing lights, and no walking billboards to hassle you.

t_ch: 实话告诉你吧，这会儿我刚种完田回来，就想找个清静的地方喘口气……
t_en: Honestly, I just finished planting in my fields and was searching for a quiet place to take a breather...

t_ch: 梦里还能种田？
t_en: You can farm in a dream?

t_ch: 很稀奇吗？家族还能在梦里盖楼呢。
t_en: Is it that strange? The Family builds buildings in the dream.

t_ch: 虽然不像筑梦师那么专业，但只要决心足够坚定，任何人都可以把梦境塑造成想要的样子。你得先这样，再这样……
t_en: Though we may not be as professional as Dreamweavers, all it takes is firm determination and anyone can mold the Dreamscape into the vision they desire. First you do this, then you do that...

t_ch: 我也想要种田！
t_en: I want to farm too!

t_ch: 哟，你居然也对种田感兴趣？真是少见。
t_en: Oh, you're interested in farming, too? This is rare.

t_ch: 虽然不像筑梦师那么专业，但只要决心足够坚定，任何人都可以把梦境塑造成想要的样子。你得先这样，再这样……
t_en: Though we may not be as professional as Dreamweavers, all it takes is firm determination and anyone can mold the Dreamscape into the vision they desire. First you do this, then you do that...

t_ch: 男人兴奋地为你讲述了梦境耕耘的一百个注意事项。
t_en: The man excitedly reels off hundreds of things to look out for when farming in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 呼，难得有人愿意一口气听我说这么多——梦里的人都不喜欢种田，看来你是个可塑之才啊！
t_en: Phew, it's rare to find someone willing to listen to me sharing all of this... No one in the dream enjoys farming. But it seems that you could be someone I could work with!

t_ch: 我叫梅隆，要是你对种田感兴趣的话，欢迎随时来找我探讨。
t_en: My name is Mellon. If you're interested in farming, you're welcome to come and discuss it with me whenever you please.

t_ch: 我来找你聊天了。
t_en: I'd like to chat.

t_ch: 正好田里没活，你想要聊什么？
t_en: I actually don't have any plants to tend to at the moment. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 聊聊你的田。
t_en: I want to talk about your fields.

t_ch: 我的田…它设在流梦礁城外，一处偏僻的梦境角落…原本我想过去「绿洲的时刻」选址，但被猎犬拒绝了。
t_en: My fields... They're located outside the Dreamflux Reef city, in a remote corner of the Dreamscape... I originally wanted to find land in the Moment of Oasis, but the Hounds refused me.

t_ch: 那些受欢迎的梦里可没有空间给人种田——像什么「黄金的时刻」，早就被高楼挤满了。
t_en: Those popular dream areas don't have enough space to allow people fields to farm in... Places like the Golden Hour have long since been filled up by giant buildings.

t_ch: 当然，梦里种田也得讲究家族法。十二时刻的时间都被锁死了，只有在流梦礁，作物才会生长。
t_en: Of course, you have to pay attention to the laws of The Family if you want to plant fields in a dream. The time in the Twelve Hours is frozen, and crops will only grow in the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 这地方也没阳光啊。
t_en: But this place has no sun.

t_ch: 这地方也没土壤啊。
t_en: But this place has no soil.

t_ch: 这地方也没水和肥料啊。
t_en: But this place has no water or fertilizer.

t_ch: …啊哈哈，倒也没有那么拟真。每天浇一遍苏乐达就够了。
t_en: ...Ahaha, farming here is not that realistic. I just have to water them with SoulGlad once a day and they're good.

t_ch: 可惜，现在大家都用合成机生产粮食，谁又能理解一分耕耘一分收获的快乐呢……
t_en: Unfortunately, everyone uses synthesizers to produce food nowadays... No one understands the joy of "reaping what you sow" anymore...

t_ch: 你为什么留在流梦礁？
t_en: Why have you decided to stay in the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我在现实里是星际和平公司的一员，年终奖抽中了来匹诺康尼的旅游机会……
t_en: I will not hide it from you. In the real world, I am a member of the IPC. I won a ticket to travel to Penacony in the end-of-year raffle...

t_ch: 原本以为最火的「黄金的时刻」会足够有新意，谁曾想那地方跟其他都市星球没什么区别——都太城市化了。
t_en: Originally, I thought that the Golden Hour that everyone loves so much would be exciting enough for me. What I didn't expect was that it would be no different to any other urban planet... Far too urbanized for me.

t_ch: 城市化不好吗？
t_en: Is urbanization not a good thing?

t_ch: 你似乎有些…
t_en: You seem a little...

t_ch: 别误会，我可是公司员工，怎么会认为城市不好呢？可如果连旅游都只能看见千篇一律的景观，未免太无聊了些。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. I am an IPC member, so how could I believe that urbanization is a bad thing? However, if even my travel destinations offer nothing but the same scenery, I'm going to find it dull.

t_ch: 所以，我一直想要去一个和庇尔波因特不同的地方。这流梦礁…算是实现了一半吧。
t_en: So, I always wanted to go somewhere different to Pier Point. With the Dreamflux Reef... I guess I've half-achieved that.

t_ch: 先聊这么多。
t_en: Let's leave it here for now.

t_ch: 没事，我很高兴有人愿意听我说话。
t_en: Alright! I'm very happy to have someone willing to listen to me.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 那再见，我做点别的事情打发时间。
t_en: Farewell. I shall find something else to kill the time.

t_ch: 哟，是你啊。
t_en: Ah, it's you.

t_ch: 田地是最公平的，有劳才有得。不干活，就没饭吃。
t_en: The farms are the definition of fairness. If you want to benefit, you have to do the work. If you don't work, you'll have nothing to eat.

t_ch: 现实也是一样。有些事情，不把脚踏进泥里，就永远无法做成…越是参加劳动，对这样的道理感悟也越深刻。
t_en: The real world is the same. There are some things that you will never be able to achieve if you don't get your hands dirty... The more you get involved in real labor, the more you will understand this truth.

t_ch: 唉，这样的日子还能持续多久呢？
t_en: Ah, how much longer can the days continue like this?

t_ch: 忧虑笼罩了梅隆的脸庞，仿佛田地上空的乌云。
t_en: Worry begins to cover Mellon's face like dark clouds over a field.

t_ch: 你应该也听说了，流梦礁正在逐渐崩塌。或许过不了多久，这地方就会消失。到时候梦里就只剩下那呆板的十二时刻可去了。
t_en: You must have heard that the Dreamflux Reef is gradually collapsing. There is a chance that this place will disappear very soon. When that time comes, the dream will only have the boring Twelve Hours to offer.

t_ch: 更难顶的是，这地方一时半会儿又毁不掉。就像你明明得了绝症，却偏偏是早期，距离发作还有几年活头……
t_en: What's worse is that this place will still not be destroyed for a while. It's like being diagnosed with a terminal illness, but in the early stages. Like you still have a few years before it takes effect...

t_ch: 抱歉，扯远了…唉，好不容易才找到可以种地的梦境。这样的日子还能持续多久呢？
t_en: Sorry, I got carried away... Ah, I finally managed to find a Dreamscape that allows me to tend to my fields. How much longer can days like this continue?

t_ch: 啧，过上男耕女织的生活就这么难吗？
t_en: Tsk, is it really this hard to enjoy an old-fashioned, traditional life?

t_ch: 梅隆的眉头紧皱成一团，仿佛隆起的田埂。
t_en: Mellon's brows furrow into a frown, like the raised banks of a cultivated field.

t_ch: 我之所以这么热衷于劳动，是因为我出生在一个农业星球。那里环境不好，必须经常开垦新的田地、精耕细作，不然就会闹饥荒。
t_en: The only reason I am so passionate about hard labor like this is because I was born on a farming planet. The environment there is poor and new fields must be cultivated constantly and carefully to avoid famine.

t_ch: 自从并入星际贸易网络后，原本能用来耕作的土地都被铺上了高速路，粮食供应也被星际和平公司一手包揽。再没有饥荒出现，也没有值得开垦的地方了。
t_en: Ever since we were integrated into the Interstellar trade network, all land that could be used for farming has been paved over with highways and the IPC has taken over our food supplies. There is no more risk of hunger and no more land is worth farming.

t_ch: 可惜，工厂终究不像田里那样令人安心。我加入公司、来到宇宙，就是为了找到可以平静生活的地方……
t_en: Unfortunately, factories are nowhere near as comforting to work in as the fields. I joined the IPC and travelled the universe just so that I could find a place to live a quiet life...

t_ch: 没想到泛银河市场里的星球基本都被开发了个遍，仅剩的几片净土也都是漫天要价的生态旅游区…只有在梦里，才能找到免费的处女地。
t_en: What I did not expect is that basically every planet in the pan-cosmic market has been completely developed, and the few remaining areas of untouched land are ecotourism areas with ridiculous prices... It's only inside a dream that I can find free, untouched land.

t_ch: 啧…我只是想体验男耕女织的简单生活，为什么就这么难呢！
t_en: Tsk... I just want to experience a simple, traditional life. Why should that be so hard!?

t_ch: 梅隆的眉头紧皱成一团，仿佛隆起的田埂。
t_en: Mellon's brows furrow into a frown, like the raised banks of a cultivated field.

t_ch: 泛银河市场里的星球基本都被开发了个遍，仅剩的几片净土也都是漫天要价的生态旅游区…只有在梦里，才能找到免费的处女地。
t_en: Every planet in the pan-cosmic market has been completely developed, and the few remaining areas of untouched land are ecotourism areas with ridiculous prices... It's only inside a dream that I can find free, untouched land.

t_ch: 啧…我只是想体验男耕女织的简单生活，为什么就这么难呢！
t_en: Tsk... I just want to experience a simple, traditional life. Why should that be so hard!?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，田里怎么能长出钞票来呢？
t_en: Hahaha, how would money grow in the land?

t_ch: …说起来，曾经有个皮皮西人也对种田表示过好奇，还兴高采烈地让我带他去参观。
t_en: ...That reminds me, there was a Pepeshi person who was very curious about farming before, too. He even excitedly asked me to show him around the field.

t_ch: 一问才知道，他居然想把苜蓿钞种在地里，还说过上几年就能成为梦境首富。我拗不过他，就圈了一块地让他自己捣鼓……
t_en: It was only when I asked him that I learned he planned to plant Alfalfa credits in the land... He said that in a few years, he would be a Dreamscape millionaire. I couldn't convince him otherwise, so I circled off some land for him to play with...

t_ch: 结果第二年挖开一看，别说长出钞票来，连原本那些钞票都不翼而飞了。
t_en: In the end, when he came to dig it up the next year, not only were there no banknotes to harvest... Even the ones he originally planted had vanished without a trace.

t_ch: 所以说啊，种田也要讲究家族法！
t_en: That's why I say you have to abide by The Family's rules even when farming!

t_ch: …说起来，曾经有个皮皮西人也对种田表示过好奇，还兴高采烈地让我带他去参观。
t_en: ...That reminds me, there was a Pepeshi person who was very curious about farming before, too. He even excitedly asked me to show him around the field.

t_ch: 一问才知道，他居然想把苜蓿钞种在地里，还说过上几年就能成为梦境首富。我拗不过他，就圈了一块地让他自己捣鼓……
t_en: It was only when I asked him that I learned he planned to plant Alfalfa credits in the land... He said that in a few years, he would be a Dreamscape millionaire. I couldn't convince him otherwise, so I circled off some land for him to play with...

t_ch: 结果第二年挖开一看，别说长出钞票来，连原本那些钞票都不翼而飞了。
t_en: In the end, when he came to dig it up the next year, not only were there no banknotes to harvest... Even the ones he originally planted had vanished without a trace.

t_ch: 所以说啊，种田也要讲究家族法！
t_en: That's why I say you have to abide by The Family's rules even when farming!

t_ch: 若鸟儿生来并无镣铐，又是何物束缚我命运……
t_en: Birds are born with no shackles, then what fetters my fate?

t_ch: 这一句要舒缓地唱，就像星星在夜空中缓慢亮起，船只从月光照耀的港湾出航……
t_en: This line should be sung gently, like stars slowly lighting up in the night sky, or like a ship slowly setting sail from a moonlit harbor...

t_ch: 在她温柔的哼唱下，熟悉的旋律仿佛蒙上了不一样的色彩。她注意到你的到来，微笑着向你点头致意。
t_en: Under the gentle tone of her humming, this familiar melody seems to take on a whole new meaning. As she notices your arrival, she smiles and nods her head to greet you.

t_ch: 您在唱什么？
t_en: What is that you were singing?

t_ch: 歌手知更鸟闻名银河的作品，《使一颗心免于哀伤》。
t_en: It's a famous piece by Robin, a singer known across the cosmos: If I Can Stop One Heart From Breaking.

t_ch: 您是歌手吗？
t_en: Are you a singer?

t_ch: 噢不，我只是一位退休的音乐教师。
t_en: Oh, no. I'm just a retired music teacher.

t_ch: 洁白的花瓣飘飞远逝~
t_en: Blown away, the white petals~

t_ch: 呵呵，多动听啊，年轻人的歌声果然充满了活力。
t_en: Hehe. You have a lovely voice. The voices of the young are always so full of vitality.

t_ch: 自从退休之后，我就在这里教孩子们唱歌，刚刚我正在备课呢。
t_en: Ever since I retired, I have taught children here to sing. I was just preparing a lesson.

t_ch: 孩子们来自阿斯德纳星系的各处，有空便通过入梦装置聚在这里。虽然在现实中他们可能永远无法拥有接受音乐教育的机会，但在梦中我们得以自由自在地歌唱。
t_en: Children from all over the Asdana star system use dream devices to gather here when they get time. Though they may never have opportunities to receive musical education in the real world, they can sing freely as they please here in the dream.

t_ch: 《使一颗心免于哀伤》深受孩子们喜爱，我们最近正在逐字逐句地打磨与练习。希望有一天，现实中的人们也能听见他们动人的歌声。
t_en: If I Can Stop One Heart From Breaking is very popular with the children. Recently, we have been practicing and focusing on every word and syllable, in the hopes that one day the real world will get to hear their beautiful voices.

t_ch: 期待着那一天的到来。
t_en: I look forward to that day.

t_ch: 在大家的帮助下，孩子们进步得飞快。不久之后，他们也许就能进行完整的演唱了。
t_en: The children have been improving quickly thanks to everyone's help. They may even be ready for a full recital soon.

t_ch: 我可以加入吗？
t_en: Can I join, too?

t_ch: 当然可以！知识理应在梦境中自由流通，每个人都有接受教育的权利与机会。
t_en: Of course! Knowledge should flow freely in the dreamscape. Everyone should have the rights and opportunity to receive education.

t_ch: 感谢您的付出。
t_en: Thank you for your hard work.

t_ch: 我的付出不值一提，是梦境之中所有人共同的努力才使得这些贫苦的孩子们拥有了受教育的机会。
t_en: My hard work is nothing really. It is the hard work of everyone in the Dreamscape that has allowed these poor children to receive an opportunity to be educated.

t_ch: 梦境是虚拟的，但这虚拟的空间却给予了我们跨越距离相遇的机会。
t_en: The dreamscape may be virtual, but this virtual space has given us the opportunity to traverse distance and come together.

t_ch: 即便是残翼的鸟儿，在这梦中，亦有飞翔的可能。为了这一天，我会一直守望下去。
t_en: Even a bird with clipped wings has the potential to fly in a dream. I will always be watching over them, so that they may see this day.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 没问题，和孩子们正式排练的时间还早。年轻人，你想聊些什么？
t_en: No problem. It is still too early to arrange a formal rehearsal with the children. What did you want to talk about, youngster?

t_ch: 为什么想要帮助这些孩子？
t_en: Why do you want to help these children?

t_ch: 要说这个念头何时萌发的，可能要追溯到我第一次来到阿斯德纳星系的时候。
t_en: If you mean where this idea first originated from, I would say it was likely from when I first came to the Asdana star system.

t_ch: 我第一次看到了梦的具象化，神奇的联觉梦境将身处不同空间的人们联系在一起，创造出种种奇观。
t_en: It was the first time I saw the materialization of a dream. The mysterious Synesthesia Dreamscape connected people from different spaces together to create all sorts of wonders.

t_ch: 令人遗憾的是，联觉梦境变成了纸醉金迷的美梦，而我从那时便想，如果大家能够来到虚拟空间中分享知识和技能，该有多好。
t_en: Regrettably, the Synesthesia Dreamscape quickly transformed into a dream of indulgence and luxury. It was then that I thought how amazing it would be if everyone was able to gather in a virtual space to share their knowledge and skills.

t_ch: 在我退休之后，我终于能够着手去利用梦境的功能，让现实中贫苦的孩子们接受音乐教育。
t_en: After I retired, I was finally able to make use of the Dreamscape's functions and provide musical education to impoverished children in the real world.

t_ch: 梦境对您来说意味着什么？
t_en: What does the Dreamscape mean to you?

t_ch: 梦境么…对我来说，它是现实的一种补充。
t_en: The Dreamscape... For me, it is a supplement to the real world.

t_ch: 在现实中，我因为车祸失去了行动的能力，但在梦中，我却能够跨越遥远的距离与孩子们相遇。
t_en: In the real world, I lost the ability to move because of a traffic accident. But, in the dream, I am able to traverse vast distances to meet with these children.

t_ch: 但我们在梦中所做的一切，都是为了反哺现实——我们在梦中做好准备，从而在现实中更好地飞翔。
t_en: However, everything that we do in the dream world is to enrich ourselves in reality... We prepare ourselves in the dream that we may fly higher in the real world.

t_ch: 平时有遇到什么难题吗？
t_en: Are there any troubles you've faced along the way?

t_ch: 入梦装置的价格、我身体的病痛、孩子们的身体状况…乃至于家族的举动，都会对我们这一方小小的合唱团造成巨大的影响。
t_en: The price of dream devices, my affliction and symptoms, the children's physical conditions... and even the actions of The Family have a huge influence on our tiny little chorus.

t_ch: 所幸在大家的帮助下，这些困难没有打倒我们。未来也许会有更困难的时候，但我坚信只要我们不放弃，一定能够安然度过。
t_en: Fortunately, thanks to everyone's help, these challenges did not bring us down. There may be even greater challenges in the future, but I firmly believe that as long as we don't give up, we will be able to make it through safely.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 那么我就继续备课了。
t_en: Then I'll return to prepping for class.

t_ch: 年轻人，愿你们在大海静谧的怀抱中安享美梦之后，为世界献上一束破晓的光。再见。
t_en: Youngster, I wish that you would offer a ray of light to the world after enjoying the sweet dream in the silent embrace of the ocean. Farewell.

t_ch: 孩子们一直在稳定地进步，他们的潜力多么大啊……
t_en: The children have been making steady progress, and they all have such amazing potential...

t_ch: 在现实中，这些孩子往往都身负某些缺陷，他们或不善与人交往，或自小患有某种疾病…总之，他们是生活在社会暗面的边缘人。
t_en: In reality, all of these children suffer with some kind of disadvantage. Some of them struggle with social interaction, some of them have struggled with their health from a young age... Whatever it is, they are all marginalized people that live in the shadows of society.

t_ch: 自从在球笼事故中失去行动能力之后，我明白了一件事——相比于怜悯和同情，他们更需要的是尊重和平等对待。
t_en: Ever since I lost my ability to move in a Spheroid accident, I've come to realize something... What they need more than pity and sympathy is respect and equal treatment.

t_ch: 在与他们一起训练的过程中，我们遭遇了无数困难，灰心丧气、中途退出、被误解、被拒绝…甚至恶语相向——但他们终究没有辜负自己的努力。
t_en: In the process of training with them, we have encountered countless hardships. Becoming disheartened, giving up, being misunderstood, being rejected... We have even exchanged harsh words... But in the end, they never gave up on all their hard work.

t_ch: 每次看到他们互相配合，快乐地沉浸在音乐之中，不断向着更好的自己前进的时候，我就像目睹奇迹一般幸福……
t_en: Every time I see them working together, happily immersed in the music and constantly moving towards a better version of themselves... The joy I feel is like I'm watching a miracle unfold before me.

t_ch: 除了音乐之外，孩子们也应该接触其他的学科……
t_en: Children should be exposed to other subjects beside music, too.

t_ch: 艺术能够帮助人们更好地面对生活中的种种苦难，但毕竟无法直接解决实际的生活问题。
t_en: Art can allow people to better face the various challenges of life, though it cannot directly solve our real-world problems.

t_ch: 我计划在接下来的日子里，与其他退休的老师一起，为孩子们开设更加系统的课程。
t_en: In the coming future, I plan to work with other retired teachers to put together a more systematic syllabus.

t_ch: 年轻人，你觉得孩子们会对什么样的学科感兴趣？
t_en: Youngster, what kind of subjects do you think children are interested in?

t_ch: 需要动手实践的学科。
t_en: Subjects that require hands-on experience.

t_ch: 例如机械制造、园艺、烹饪这样的课程…是的，它们非常实用。
t_en: You mean like engineering, gardening, cooking...? You're right. They've been very practical subjects.

t_ch: 需要思维训练的学科。
t_en: Subjects that require mental training.

t_ch: 例如逻辑、数学这样的课程…是的，它们能极大地提升思维能力。
t_en: You mean like logic and mathematics...? You're right. That will help them to greatly improve their thinking skills.

t_ch: 说不准，应该因材施教。
t_en: Maybe they should be taught according to their individual skills?

t_ch: 有道理，孩子们的天赋不同，擅长的东西也不同，能够扬长避短是最好的。
t_en: You may have a point. Children have different talents and excel in different areas. It would be best to utilize their strengths and avoid their weaknesses.

t_ch: 可惜我们人手太少，一时之间找不到更多的教师。
t_en: It's a shame that we are so understaffed and cannot find any more teachers at the moment.

t_ch: 听说博识学会向来以传播知识为使命，也许我应该联系他们。感谢你的意见，年轻人。
t_en: I hear that the mission of the Intelligentsia Guild has always been to spread knowledge. Maybe I should contact them? Thank you for your thoughts, youngster.

t_ch: 我不明白，为什么总有人当着孩子的面对他们的努力冷嘲热讽！
t_en: I have never understood how there are people that can stare a child in the eye while mocking their hard work!

t_ch: 有许多人并不认可我的所作所为，他们相信无论如何你都不可能将劣质的种子培育成参天大树。
t_en: There are many people that do not approve of what I am doing. They believe that no matter what you do, a damaged seed can never be cultivated into a great tree.

t_ch: 我能理解这样的悲观预期，但我不明白，他们为什么要当面打击孩子的自信心！
t_en: While I understand their pessimistic outlooks, I don't understand why they would attack a child's self-confidence in front of them like that!

t_ch: 曾有一个孩子好不容易鼓起勇气唱出自己的声音，却被人当面嘲讽难听。回去之后，我便再也没有见过她。
t_en: I had a child who worked really hard to gather her courage and sing out loud, yet people laughed at her and told her she was terrible. I have not seen her again since she left.

t_ch: 从始至终，我希望的从不是他们长成别人眼里的参天大树，而是他们能够拥有像普通人一样的生活，能够在悲伤的时候、喜悦的时候想起音乐，想起那些充满希望的事物——
t_en: From the very beginning, my aspiration was not for them to grow into what others see as vast, towering trees. It was for them to be able to enjoy the life of a normal person. That when they were happy or sad, they would be able to think of music and think of those moments full of hope...

t_ch: 唉，就连这样微小的愿望，也有人无情地践踏！
t_en: Ah... There are people out there that would heartlessly trample even a tiny little hope like this!

t_ch: 我还能陪伴他们多久……
t_en: I wonder how much longer I can accompany them...

t_ch: 受伤之后，我的身体一天不如一天了，最近就连入梦的时候也会感觉疲惫。
t_en: Since I was injured, my health has been worsening by the day. Recently, I've been feeling fatigued even when entering the dream.

t_ch: 虽然平时在训练的时候，我常常有意无意和孩子们开玩笑，老师要是突然消失了该怎么办，但他们总是嘻嘻哈哈地应付过去。
t_en: Even though I'm constantly joking with them about "what if the teacher suddenly disappears one day" during our practices, they always just laugh it off.

t_ch: 也许下次该让孩子们学习一些悲伤的歌曲，毕竟这是无可回避的人生话题。总有一天他们会飞向天空，而我会坠落大地——
t_en: Maybe next time I should teach the children some sad songs. This is a life topic that cannot be avoided forever, after all. One day, they will soar toward the sky, and I will fall to the ground...

t_ch: 但悲伤和眼泪令幸福与微笑显得如此珍贵，我们用美将哀痛的事实包裹，吞咽下去，而这就是艺术能带给我们的力量。
t_en: But it is sadness and tears that make smiles and joy all the more precious. We wrap our sadness and suffering in beautiful packaging and swallow them. That is the power that art can bring us.

t_ch: 最近到底发生了什么……
t_en: What's going on these days...

t_ch: 忆质的流动速度变得越来越快，越来越快……
t_en: The speed of the memoria flow is growing faster...

t_ch: 你在说什么？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 流梦礁的忆质， 越来越不正常了……
t_en: It seems like the Dreamflux Reef's memoria is becoming increasingly abnormal...

t_ch: 你怎么知道？
t_en: Wait, how did you know?

t_ch: 长期观察结合精密的测量，我得出的结果应该不会有多大误差。
t_en: Based on my long-term observation and precise measurements, I'm sure my result shouldn't have many errors.

t_ch: 我也感觉到了！
t_en: I felt it too!

t_ch: 真的吗？看来你的灵敏度堪比精密的科学仪器。
t_en: Is that true? It looks like your sensitivity is comparable to a precision scientific instrument.

t_ch: 不对，你是不是在和我开玩笑…算了，我都习惯了。
t_en: No way. Are you messing around with me...? Never mind. You're not the only one anyway.

t_ch: 自我开始研究忆质动力学以来，我就一直在监测阿斯德纳忆域的忆质流动情况。
t_en: Since I started my research on memoria dynamics, I've been monitoring the flow of memoria in the Asdana Memory Zone.

t_ch: 不对…肯定有什么怪东西在影响着流梦礁……
t_en: It can't be... There's definitely something strange affecting the Dreamflux Reef...

t_ch: 不好意思，我要继续观测了……
t_en: Please excuse me. I need to continue my observation...

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 时间宝贵，请尽可能长话短说。
t_en: Time is precious. Please keep it concise.

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我曾是来自折纸大学的学者，专业是忆质动力学。为了完成我的课题，我一直在流梦礁工作。
t_en: I'm a scholar from the Paperfold University, specializing in memoria dynamics. To complete my research, I have been working in the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 除了学者的身份之外，我也经常在流梦礁开设关于忆质的科普活动，担任孩子们的科学教师。
t_en: Besides being a scholar, I often run science programs on memoria in the Dreamflux Reef and serve as a science teacher for the kids.

t_ch: 忆质动力学是什么？
t_en: What is memoria dynamics?

t_ch: 忆质就像是精神领域的液体，它会流动，蒸发，汇聚，它能溶解、沸腾、凝固。它能够影响意识，塑造幻觉，产生梦境……
t_en: Memoria is like a fluid on the mental dimension. It will flow, evaporate, and converge. It can dissolve, boil, and solidify. It can influence consciousness, shape illusions, and produce dreams...

t_ch: 所谓的忆质动力学，便是研究其运动规律并加以应用的学科。
t_en: And so, memoria dynamics is the study of its laws of motion and their application.

t_ch: 流梦礁发生什么了？
t_en: What is happening to the Dreamflux Reef?

t_ch: 简单来说，组成它的忆质在不断流失——终有一天，它会消融在忆域的深海之中。
t_en: Simply put, the memoria that forms it is depleting. One day, it will dissolve in the depths of the Memory Zone's sea.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 虽然我还没有得出精确的结论，但是种种迹象都表明，流梦礁正变得越来越危险。
t_en: While I haven't reached a definitive conclusion yet, all signs suggest that Dreamflux Reef is growing increasingly perilous.

t_ch: 还请快些离开吧。
t_en: So please, leave while you can.

t_ch: 流梦礁…就要消失了吗……
t_en: Is the Dreamflux Reef... going to disappear...?

t_ch: 忆域就像一片海洋，流梦礁便是其中一座渺小的岛屿。
t_en: The Memory Zone is like a sea, and the Dreamflux Reef is only a tiny island on it.

t_ch: 但如果组成岛屿的忆质不断流失，那么终有一天它会消融在忆域之中。
t_en: However, if the memoria that is forming the island is constantly depleting, the island will inevitably dissolve in the depths of Memory Zone one day.

t_ch: 她在这里度过了短暂而绚烂的一生，难道就要这样葬身于无底的时间之中，与荒蛮的迷因相伴吗？
t_en: She has spent a short and splendid life in this place. Is she going to be buried here for eternity in bottomless time, and be accompanied by nothing but savage memes?

t_ch: 这实在是太不公平了……
t_en: This is too unfair...

t_ch: 只要找出原因，也许就能扭转忆质的异常活动。
t_en: As long as we can find out the cause, we could perhaps reverse the abnormal activity of the memoria.

t_ch: 流梦礁的忆质都在朝向黑洞那端涌去……
t_en: The Dreamflux Reef's memoria is flowing toward the Black Hole...

t_ch: 如果我们能够找到诱发忆质如此运动的事物，并且找到办法消除它，那么一切也许还来得及。
t_en: If we can find the thing that induces such a movement of memoria and discover a way to eliminate it, it may not be too late to savage the situation.

t_ch: 听说家族邀请了众多派系的宾客来到美梦，但愿有人能够发现这一点，并且去阻止吧。
t_en: I heard that The Family has invited many guests from different factions to the dream. Let us hope that someone can discover this and stop it in time.

t_ch: 我来阻止！
t_en: Let me do it!

t_ch: 并非是我想打击您，想凭一个人的力量扭转忆质流动的趋势太困难了。
t_en: Not that I wish to put you down, but it will be tough to try to reverse the flow of memoria single-handedly.

t_ch: 总有人会解决。
t_en: Someone will eventually do it.

t_ch: 但愿吧……
t_en: Let's hope so...

t_ch: 也许谁都阻止不了。
t_en: Maybe no one can stop it.

t_ch: 只要付出了自己的全力，也许什么结局我都能接受吧。
t_en: As long as I did my best, whatever the outcome is, I'll accept it.

t_ch: 许多人忽视了她的贡献，这绝对违反了学术的良心！
t_en: Many ignore her contributions, and this is an absolute violation of the academic conscience!

t_ch: 曾有这样一位女性，是她首创了对于忆质运动的科学研究。
t_en: There was a woman who pioneered the scientific study of memoria movements.

t_ch: 她是「钟表匠」的伙伴，在灾难中的岁月为美梦无私奉献，留下诸多珍贵的手稿，而后籍籍无名地死去。
t_en: She was the Watchmaker's companion and selflessly dedicated herself to the dream during years of disasters, leaving behind numerous precious manuscripts, yet she passed away without any recognition.

t_ch: 在折纸大学，忆质动力学的课本里居然连她的名字都没有，取而代之的是所谓的学术权威——她无力为自己辩解，但这并不是他们篡夺学术的借口！
t_en: At the Paperfold University, her name isn't even mentioned in the memoria dynamics textbook. Instead, it was replaced by the so-called academic authorities — she cannot defend herself, but this is no excuse for their usurpation of her works!

t_ch: 因为受够了那些所谓权威的恶劣行径，我来到流梦礁探寻她的足迹……
t_en: Because I had enough of the bad behavior of those so-called authorities, I came to the Dreamflux Reef in search of her footsteps...

t_ch: 可恶的剽窃者，你们不能欺瞒所有人！
t_en: Evil plagiarists, you cannot fool everyone!

t_ch: 能够花这么多时间在自己感兴趣的研究话题之上——回过头想象，也是一种幸福……
t_en: Come to think about it... I consider myself lucky to be able to spend so much time on a research topic that interests me...

t_ch: 我在折纸大学研究忆质动力学，那是个很难的学科，建系以来也只有寥寥不过百人毕业。
t_en: I studied memoria dynamics, a very challenging subject, at Paperfold University, and only a few hundred people have graduated since the department was founded.

t_ch: 我的师兄师姐里有很多是被调剂来这个专业的，但我是真心喜欢关于「记忆」的世界，我喜欢它澄澈明净的时候，也喜欢它神秘无常、波涛汹涌的时候。
t_en: Many of my seniors were transferred to this specialization, but I genuinely love the world of "Remembrance." Whether they are transparent and clean or mysterious and unpredictable, I love them all equally.

t_ch: 虽然不是忆者，但我也渴望了解忆质的本质，渴望探寻生命所能抵达的深度。
t_en: I may not be a Memokeeper, but I too am curious to find out more about the nature of memoria and explore the depths to which life can reach.

t_ch: 在这一路上，我遇到了许多震撼人心的事与人，成群结队如同光影一般闪烁的忆者，庞大到令人窒息的忆域迷因，在忆质的潮水中退守最后家园的人们……
t_en: Along the way, I encountered many stunning things and people, such as hordes of Memokeepers who shimmered like lights and shadows, gigantic Memory Zone memes suffocating in their size, and people who retreated to and guarded their last homes in the tidal wave of memoria...

t_ch: 那些旅途的孤独和痛苦，回头望去也许是上天的馈赠——它将痛苦教给我们，同时也教会了我们从痛苦中感受幸福的能力。
t_en: Looking back at the loneliness and pain in these journeys, they may have been a gift from heaven — to teach us pain as well as the ability to feel happiness from those painful moments.

t_ch: 我会一直坚守在这里，为了我自己的理想……
t_en: I am going to always stay here... for my ideal...

t_ch: …也为了她的心愿。
t_en: ...And for her wish.

t_ch: 那股影响流梦礁忆质的异常力量似乎消失了……
t_en: That abnormal energy influencing the memoria of Dreamflux Reef seems to have disappeared...

t_ch: 这样一来，流梦礁能够缓慢地修复自身，也许有一天，它会变回从前那个繁华的样子。
t_en: In that case, the Dreamflux Reef can gradually recover itself. Perhaps one day, it will return to prosperity.

t_ch: 不过是谁做了这一切呢？
t_en: But who is the one behind it?

t_ch: 一定是某个大英雄。
t_en: They must be a great hero.

t_ch: 是啊，如果没有人挺身而出，这样棘手的问题绝不会自己消失。
t_en: Yes. If no one stands out and takes action, such a difficult situation will not resolve by itself.

t_ch: 一定是很多人共同的努力。
t_en: I'm sure it's a team effort.

t_ch: 是啊，面对如此棘手的问题，汇聚众人的力量才有解决的可能。
t_en: Yes. When facing with such a difficult situation, only the power of teamwork can resolve it.

t_ch: 是我，银河球棒侠！
t_en: It's me, the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 原来是你…不愧是和拉扎莉娜女士一样的无名客啊！
t_en: So it's you... Not bad for a Nameless like Madam Razalina!

t_ch: 无论是谁，我衷心感谢所有帮助过流梦礁的人。虽然我并非这里的原住民，但流梦礁几乎已经是我第二个故乡了。
t_en: Regardless, I am sincerely grateful to those who helped the Dreamflux Reef. I may not be a local of this place, but the Dreamflux Reef has almost been my second hometown.

t_ch: 作为学者，我也知道自己有时不合时宜，有时过于死板严谨，甚至令人不可理喻——但流梦礁的人们包容了我的缺点，为我的研究提供了许多方便。
t_en: As a scholar, I acknowledge that at times I may have acted inappropriately, overly rigid, stringent, or even incomprehensible — yet the people of the Dreamflux Reef have embraced my shortcomings and supported my research in numerous ways.

t_ch: 无论如何，我不会忘记这里的人们，和这里曾发生的故事。毕竟，研究忆质的人，永远不会轻慢那些美好的记忆……
t_en: No matter what, I'll never forget the people and the stories here. After all, people who study memoria will never take those beautiful memories for granted.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 时间宝贵，请尽可能长话短说。
t_en: Time is precious. Please keep it concise.

t_ch: 现在流梦礁的情况怎么样？
t_en: How's the situation of the Dreamflux Reef now?

t_ch: 根据我的观测，流梦礁的忆质不再向着美梦的方向涌流。
t_en: Based on my observation, the Dreamflux Reef's memoria is no longer flowing toward sweeter dreams.

t_ch: 这就意味着忆质会重新盈满流梦礁被腐蚀的空洞——如果能得到恰当的建设，那么它总有一天会变得繁荣而美丽。
t_en: This means the memoria will replenish the corroded void of Dreamflux Reef, and if it is properly constructed, the Dreamflux Reef will flourish and become beautiful one day.

t_ch: 你接下来的计划是什么？
t_en: What's your next plan?

t_ch: 我完成了自己的课题，关于忆质的论文也马上要出版了。
t_en: I have completed my research, and my paper on memoria is also about to be published.

t_ch: 也许我会离开流梦礁前往忆域中更加深邃的地方，但在这之前，我想再好好搜集与研究拉扎莉娜的手稿。每次阅读，我总会有新的想法，新的灵感……
t_en: Perhaps I'll leave the Dreamflux Reef and travel to a deeper place in the Memory Zone, but before I do, I'd like to gather and study Razalina's manuscripts again. Every time I read them, I'll get new ideas and new inspirations...

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 再见，愿我们都能在彼此的旅途中见到壮美的风景。
t_en: Goodbye. May we all see magnificent scenery on each other's journeys.

t_ch: 流梦礁的调查研究告一段落，是留下继续研究拉扎莉娜的手稿，还是前往别处研究新的话题……
t_en: The investigation and study of the Dreamflux Reef have come to an end. Should I stay to continue studying Razalina's manuscripts or travel elsewhere to study new topics...?

t_ch: 当初无意间观测到流梦礁异常的忆质运动，还在寻找毕业课题的我就被此吸引，长期地驻扎在此地。
t_en: Initially, I was in search of a topic for my graduation project when I chanced upon the unusual movement of memoria in the Dreamflux Reef. I was deeply attracted to it and stayed here for a long time.

t_ch: 流梦礁给了我许多美好的记忆，我的研究成果也即将发表。
t_en: The Dreamflux Reef has given me many beautiful memories, and I am also about to publish my research.

t_ch: 是留下继续研究拉扎莉娜的手稿，还是前往别处研究新的话题…人生未来的方向，我得好好想想。
t_en: Should I stay to continue studying Razalina's manuscripts or travel elsewhere to study new topics...? I must think about my future and my life direction.

t_ch: 流梦礁得救了，但有些老朋友终究还是不在了……
t_en: The Dreamflux Reef may be saved, but some of my old friends are no longer around...

t_ch: 在驻留流梦礁的这段时间里，我结识了许多朋友。
t_en: I had made many friends during my stay in the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 他们不介意我的怪异，时常鼓励我进行研究，与我倾诉他们所知的过往。
t_en: They embraced my quirks without reservation and occasionally spurred me on in my research endeavors, sharing with me glimpses of the past as they remembered it.

t_ch: 如果他们能够看到现在的流梦礁，那该多好。
t_en: If only they could witness the Dreamflux Reef as it stands today, that would be truly remarkable.

t_ch: 我不该在那个时候对他们坦诚我的测算结果——他们在离世的时候，会对流梦礁的未来感到多么绝望啊……
t_en: I shouldn't have been honest with them about my measured results — how much despair they would have felt about the future of Dreamflux Reef when they passed on...

t_ch: 许多人忽视了她的贡献，这绝对违反了学术的良心！
t_en: Many ignore her contributions, and this is an absolute violation of the academic conscience!

t_ch: 曾有这样一位女性，是她首创了对于忆质运动的科学研究。
t_en: There was a woman who pioneered the scientific study of memoria movements.

t_ch: 她是「钟表匠」的伙伴，在灾难中的岁月为美梦无私奉献，留下诸多珍贵的手稿，而后籍籍无名地死去。
t_en: She was the Watchmaker's companion and selflessly dedicated herself to the dream during years of disasters, leaving behind numerous precious manuscripts, yet she passed away without any recognition.

t_ch: 在折纸大学，忆质动力学的课本里居然连她的名字都没有，取而代之的是所谓的学术权威——她无力为自己辩解，但这并不是他们篡夺学术的借口！
t_en: At the Paperfold University, her name isn't even mentioned in the memoria dynamics textbook. Instead, it was replaced by the so-called academic authorities — she cannot defend herself, but this is no excuse for their usurpation of her works!

t_ch: 因为受够了那些所谓权威的恶劣行径，我来到流梦礁探寻她的足迹……
t_en: Because I had enough of the bad behavior of those so-called authorities, I came to the Dreamflux Reef in search of her footsteps...

t_ch: 可恶的剽窃者，你们不能欺瞒所有人！
t_en: Evil plagiarists, you cannot fool everyone!

t_ch: 我好像做了一个很长的梦…梦中我见到了拉扎莉娜女士……
t_en: I felt I had a very long dream... In my dream, I saw Madam Razalina...

t_ch: 不知道怎么回事，前不久我好像在梦里睡了过去。
t_en: I don't know what happened, but I fell asleep in my dream a while ago.

t_ch: 那是多么美好的梦啊，我遇见了拉扎莉娜女士，她的样子与我想象中的一模一样，小小的身躯，无穷的活力，精辟的观点，优雅的言语……
t_en: It was such a beautiful dream. I met Madam Razalina, whose appearance was what I'd always imagined. Her body was petite, but she was energetic, sharp-witted, and could express herself elegantly...

t_ch: 我们一起在流梦礁闲逛，讨论着一道悬而未决的难题。不愧是拉扎莉娜女士，我们马上得出了正确的结果。
t_en: We hung out together at the Dreamflux Reef and discussed a difficult conundrum. And as expected of Madam Razalina, we quickly came up with the correct answer.

t_ch: 但就在我要把它记下来时——一股莫名的悲伤惊醒了我，我的枕头也被泪水浸湿了……
t_en: But just as I was about to jot it down... An unknown wave of sadness woke me. My pillow was also soaked with tears...

t_ch: 也许你真的见到了拉扎莉娜。
t_en: Maybe the woman you met in your dream was really Razalina.

t_ch: 多么希望是这样啊，可惜我学习的知识让我难以相信这点…但梦境本就是混杂着「神秘」的忆域，或许真的是她本人！
t_en: How much I wish that were the case. Unfortunately, my knowledge makes it hard for me to believe that... However, Dreamscapes are originally Memory Zones with Enigmata mixed in. Maybe that was really her!

t_ch: 我也想见拉扎莉娜！
t_en: I want to meet Razalina too!

t_ch: 哈哈，她的人格魅力如此超群，即便未见其面也能感受到她的活力。
t_en: Haha! What an outstanding personality she had! You could even sense her energy without meeting her.

t_ch: 你们已经在精神层面沟通过了！
t_en: You two have communicated on a spiritual level!

t_ch: 是啊，在阅读她的手稿时，我仿佛透过纸面看到了她的灵魂…也许那才是真正深刻的相遇吧。
t_en: Exactly. When I was reading her manuscripts, it was as if I could see her soul through the papers... Maybe that was genuinely a profound encounter.

t_ch: 即便那只是幻觉，每每回想起梦中的场景，我还是感到一阵幸福，好像她真的来到了我身边一样。
t_en: Even if it was just a hallucination, every time I recall the scene in my dream, I could still feel a surge of happiness as if she had come to me.

t_ch: 拉扎莉娜女士，我会继承你的遗愿走下去的……
t_en: Madam Razalina, I'll carry on where you left off...

t_ch: 你好，我是守卫维德，有什么需要帮忙的吗？
t_en: Hello, I am Guard Vader. How may I assist you?

t_ch: 诶，又是个生面孔，怎么称呼？
t_en: *sigh* Another unfamiliar face. How do I address you?

t_ch: 就叫我开拓者吧。
t_en: Just call me Trailblazer.

t_ch: 开拓？真是个好名字！我也喜欢开拓！
t_en: Trailblaze? That's a great name! I love trailblazing too.

t_ch: 银河球棒侠是也！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 挥舞着球棒打败怪物的侠客…这个形象放进动画和游戏里一定很有意思！
t_en: A hero who swings the baseball bat to defeat monsters? It must be interesting to have such a figure in animations and games!

t_ch: 路过的无名之客罢了。
t_en: Just a nameless one, passing by.

t_ch: 哈哈，这里还真有不少像你一样的人。
t_en: Haha, you're not the first one here.

t_ch: 总之，欢迎来到流梦礁参观！这里是钟表小子的摇篮，一段沧桑的往昔之梦，一个辉煌时代的落幕之地。
t_en: Anyway, welcome to the Dreamflux Reef as a guest! This is the birthplace of Clockie! It is a forlorn dream of bygone days, a place where a glorious era saw its curtain fall.

t_ch: 上电梯的时候看到那个钟表小子的雕像了吗？它是流梦礁的标志性建筑，也见证了流梦礁的兴衰——如果你是钟表小子的粉丝，千万记得与它合个影！
t_en: Did you see the statue of Clockie when you got into the elevator? It's an iconic landmark of the Dreamflux Reef and has also witnessed the rise and fall of the Dreamflux Reef... If you're a fan of Clockie, don't forget to take a picture with it!

t_ch: 我是钟表小子的超级粉丝！
t_en: I'm a super fan of Clockie!

t_ch: 这么巧，我也是！
t_en: Are you serious? Me too!

t_ch: 我和钟表小子是老朋友了。
t_en: I'm Clockie's old friend.

t_ch: 呃…我懂！谁不想要一位钟表小子一样的朋友啊！
t_en: Eh... I get it! Who doesn't want a friend like Clockie?

t_ch: 我和钟表小子不熟。
t_en: I don't know who Clockie is.

t_ch: 太遗憾了，下次让我给你推荐几集经典的钟表小子动画吧！你一定会爱上这个可爱的角色的！
t_en: What a pity! Let me recommend to you some of his classic cartoons next time! I'm sure you'll love this adorable character!

t_ch: 钟表小子动画延续至今，已经不仅仅是一部影视作品这么简单了。它承载了时代的变迁、历史的更迭，梦境的精神。
t_en: Clockie's cartoon has lasted to this day, and it is no longer just a movie and television series. It carries the change of times, the shifts of history, as well as the spirit of Dreamscapes.

t_ch: 虽然「钟表匠」先生从未亲口承认，但我相信，钟表小子的故事就是他自己的故事！也就是我们匹诺康尼的故事！
t_en: Mr. Watchmaker might not have admitted it, but I believe Clockie's stories are his own! In other words, they are the stories of us Penaconians!

t_ch: 除了担任守卫之外，我一直在搜集与钟表小子创作相关的物品，希望能在未来为钟表小子建立一座博物馆，但最重要的原画稿却始终不见踪影。
t_en: In addition to my job as a guard, I've been collecting items related to the creation of Clockie, hoping to build a museum for Clockie in the future. However, I cannot find the most important item — the original artwork of Clockie.

t_ch: 亲爱的旅客，你们走南闯北，如果有什么相关消息的话，请记得通知我一声！
t_en: Dear traveler, you must be exploring more places than me. If you've ever heard of any relevant news, please let me know!

t_ch: 我记住了！
t_en: I got it!

t_ch: 感激不尽！钟表小子博物馆建成的那天，您的名字一定会出现在感谢名单上。
t_en: Thank you so much! The day Clockie Museum is up, your name will certainly be on the gratitude list.

t_ch: 我不感兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested.

t_ch: 没事，您有空留意一下就行，定有酬谢。
t_en: It's fine. Just keep an eye out. I'll pay you for it.

t_ch: 有什么报酬吗？
t_en: And what do I get in return?

t_ch: 我家里还珍藏着钟表小子系列的大量手办，您要是找到了随您挑！
t_en: I have a large collection of Clockie figurines. Find the item and you can take your pick!

t_ch: 一不小心就跟您聊上了，瞧我这坏毛病。不耽误您的时间了，祝您玩得愉快！
t_en: Pardon me. Looks like I've held you up with my long conversation. I won't take up any more of your time. Have fun!

t_ch: 临别之前，让我们为美梦小镇加油吧！
t_en: Before you leave, let's cheer for Dreamville!

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 好啊，要是不耽误您的时间，想聊多久都行！
t_en: Sure. If you have the time, I'm more than willing to chat with you!

t_ch: 工作这么清闲吗？
t_en: Is your work really so relaxing?

t_ch: 其实我也是义务劳动。流梦礁不像美梦，想来就来，想走就走，只要不出恶性事件，一切都好说。
t_en: Honestly, this is a voluntary work. The Dreamflux Reef isn't like the sweet dreams. Here, you can come and go as you please. And as long as you don't cause nasty incidents, you can do whatever you want.

t_ch: 况且现在居民也少了，自然清闲得多。要是今天你不来找我聊天，我肯定在和一帮老家伙下棋呢！
t_en: Besides, there aren't many residents now, so my job naturally becomes easier. If you hadn't dropped by here to chat with me today, I'd probably be engaged in a game of chess with a group of elderly folks!

t_ch: 怎么喜欢上钟表小子的？
t_en: What's your reason for liking Clockie?

t_ch: 说起来也有点不好意思，小时候父母常常把我一个人留在家里，那阴冷的房间，吓人的雕像，瘆人的鸟叫…是钟表小子的声音赶走我的恐惧，陪着我一起长大。
t_en: Well, that's embarrassing. When I was a child, my parents used to leave me alone at home. There were terrifying statues displayed in that cold room with creepy birds chirping... But Clockie's voice chased away my fears and kept me company as I grew up.

t_ch: 所以对我来说，它已经不是一个动画形象了！它就像我的伙伴和家人，一个永远不会变老的守护者！
t_en: So, to me, it is no longer just an animated figure! It is like my partner and a family member, a guardian who never ages!

t_ch: 钟表小子博物馆进度如何？
t_en: How's the Clockie Museum going?

t_ch: 还在筹建之中，真是让人烦恼啊…最紧缺还是资金，以及投资人的信心。
t_en: It's still in the pipeline, which is a real pain... What we're lacking now is funding and the confidence of the investors.

t_ch: 不过我不会放弃的，就像钟表小子从来不会放弃一样！
t_en: But I won't give up, just like how Clockie never gives up!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 吃好玩好，小心忆域迷因！我也继续工作了。
t_en: Enjoy your time here and beware of the Memory Zone Memes! I should continue my work too.

t_ch: 最近有不少客人来到流梦礁，流梦礁一定会慢慢好起来的！
t_en: There are quite a number of people coming to the Dreamflux Reef recently. I'm sure this place will slowly get better!

t_ch: 不知从什么时候起，流梦礁变得越来越小，越来越破败，人们走的走，逃的逃，许多人暂别之后便再也没见过面。
t_en: I don't know when it started, but the Dreamflux Reef seems to be shrinking and becoming more dilapidated. People have left or fled, and many have never crossed paths again after a temporary farewell.

t_ch: 不过，最近有像你这样的新面孔来到了流梦礁，他们对这里感兴趣，愿意和这里的人们交流。
t_en: But there are new faces like you who have recently arrived at the Dreamflux Reef. They are interested in this place and willing to interact with the people here.

t_ch: 也许，在不久的将来，流梦礁将重新变回那个繁荣、美丽的地方，变成那个真正的美梦小镇！愿钟表小子的精神与我们同在！
t_en: Perhaps in the near future, the Dreamflux Reef will return to its vibrant and beautiful state, evolving to become that true Dreamville! May the spirit of Clockie be with us!

t_ch: 最近忆域迷因的活动变得有些频繁了，所幸没有发生什么恶性事件。
t_en: The activities of the Memory Zone Memes are becoming increasingly frequent, but fortunately, nothing bad happened.

t_ch: 流梦礁所在的地方不太稳定，经常会有忆域迷因闯入城镇。您有遇到过迷因吗？
t_en: The location of Dreamflux Reef is somewhat unstable, and Memory Zone memes often break into town. Have you ever encountered a meme?

t_ch: 遇到过，我把它打跑了。
t_en: Yes, and I fought it off.

t_ch: 您真是英明神武！但这样做难免有些危险。
t_en: What a brave and mighty hero! But that could be dangerous.

t_ch: 遇到过，我被打趴下了。
t_en: Yes, and it floored me.
